title: Sample Game
tagline: A one-line hook that makes a journalist want to keep reading.
descriptionShort: Tweet-length pitch: what the game is, who it's for, why it stands out — in one or two sentences a writer can paste straight into a post.
descriptionLong: First paragraph: the elevator pitch in prose. Set the premise and the core loop.
Second paragraph: what makes it different — the mechanic, the feeling, the hook nobody else has.
Third paragraph: context — who made it, why, and what players walk away with.
features: Headline feature one — lead with the most distinctive thing.
Feature two — a concrete, demonstrable mechanic.
Feature three — something that photographs or GIFs well.
Feature four — scope, length, or replayability.

awards.title: Replace with a real award or selection, or delete this section

quotes.text: A glowing pull-quote from coverage. Delete if you have none yet.
quotes.author: Writer Name

about: Scarlet Engineering is an indie studio that engineers emergence — systems-driven games where simple rules produce surprising play.
Replace this with the studio's story in two or three sentences.
monetizationNote: You are free to capture, stream, and monetize video of this game. No permission needed — coverage is always welcome.
